data.title: California Resources Corporation announces Offering of $400M
data.description:
• California Resources Corporation (NYSE:CRC)  stated a private offering of $400M in senior unsecured notes due 2034.
  • The notes will be guaranteed by the company's existing subsidiaries that guarantee its revolving credit facility, its 7.125% senior unsecured notes due 2026 and its 8.250% senior unsecured notes due 2029. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from this offering to repay the existing indebtedness of Berry Corporation.

data.title: Encore Capital announces $400M senior secured notes
data.description:
•  Encore Capital Group, Inc. (NASDAQ:ECPG) announced private offering of $400M of senior secured notes due 2031.
  • The Company intends to use the proceeds from this offering to repay drawings under its revolving credit facility and to pay certain transaction fees.

data.title: OGE Energy declares $0.425 dividend
data.description:
• OGE Energy (NYSE:OGE) declares $0.425/share quarterly dividend.
  • Forward yield 3.8%
  • Payable Oct. 31; for shareholders of record Oct. 6; ex-div Oct. 6.
  • See OGE Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Comstock to acquire multifamily property in Maryland
data.description:
Asset manager Comstock (NASDAQ:CHCI) announced on Wednesday a purchase agreement to acquire a 400+ unit multifamily property in the City of Rockville in Montgomery County, Maryland.

As part of the agreement, the company has a 45-day due diligence period.

Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The transaction is expected to close in the fourth quarter.

"This property is consistent with our strategic growth plan, is a natural fit for our managed portfolio, and is well-positioned to contribute to future revenue growth while delivering value for our strategic partners, our residents, and our shareholders," said CEO Christopher Clemente.

data.title: Stock market advances a day after Wall Street's rally takes a pause
data.description:
Wall Street edged up Wednesday, a day after Wall Street’s three-day record-setting streak stalled.

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.2%, the Dow (DJI) +0.2% and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.3%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.14%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) was flat at 3.59%.

Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) edged lower in early trade, even after its outlook eclipsed estimates on AI data center demand.

U.S. stocks ended in the red on Tuesday, breaking from their three-day all-time high closes, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell largely reiterated his message from last week that policymakers will have a difficult path ahead on interest rates.

"Powell’s Rhode Island excursion was of more market interest. Powell deliberately noted that both inflation and unemployment are moving in the wrong direction from the Fed’s perspective," UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

"Markets struggled to keep up with the recent momentum yesterday, with the S&P 500 finally slipping back after a succession of record highs, with a couple of main drivers for this retreat. One was a reversal in tech, as there were as many questions as answers over Nvidia's high-profile $100B tie-up with OpenAI from Monday. The other was a slightly more cautious tone on the labor market by Powell," Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

But the bigger concern from an AI bubble risk perspective was whether Nvidia was now relying on investments into customers to feed its revenue growth and whether we’re beginning to see some creative accounting reminiscent of 1990s telco deals, Reid added. 

On the day ahead now, the August New Home Sales report will land during market hours. The consensus looks for a retreat to 649K in the month. 

San Francisco Federal Reserve Bank President Mary Daly will speak on the economic outlook before the annual Spencer Fox Eccles Convocation.

data.title: AstraZeneca, Daiichi Sankyo under priority review of FDA for breast cancer
data.description:
AstraZeneca (NASDAQ:AZN) and Daiichi Sankyo have received priority review from the FDA for their supplemental marketing application for Enhertu in combination with pertuzumab, marketed by Roche (OTCQX:RHHBY) as Perjeta. 

This application is aimed at treating adults with HER2-positive breast cancer that cannot be surgically removed or has spread to other parts of the body. Additionally, Enhertu has recently been designated as a breakthrough therapy by the FDA for this treatment.

The FDA action date, for their regulatory decision, is anticipated during the first quarter of 2026.

data.title: Freeport-McMoRan plunges after lowering forecast for Q3 copper, gold sales
data.description:
Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) -8.9% pre-market Wednesday after guiding for Q3 consolidated sales to come in lower by 4% for copper and 6% for gold compared with its July forecast, while updating the status of the previously reported mud rush incident at the Grasberg Block Cave mine in Indonesia, where mining operations have been temporarily suspended since September 8.

The company previously forecast consolidated sales of 1 billion lbs of copper and 350,000 oz of gold for the quarter.

Earlier this month, Freeport-McMoRan (NYSE:FCX) stopped mining at Grasberg after a large flow of wet material blocked access to parts of its underground mine, restricting evacuation routes for seven workers; the company located two team members who were killed in the incident, and said it continues to search for five team members who remain missing.

The Grasberg Block Cave ore body represents 50% of Freeport Indonesia's (FCX) estimated proven and probable reserves as of year-end 2024 and 70% of previously forecast copper and gold production through 2029.

Freeport (FCX) said it does not yet have sufficient information to forecast future production estimates, but preliminary assessments indicate the impacts likely will result in the deferral of significant production in Q4 2025 and full-year 2026 as a phased restart and ramp-up of operations begins, with a return to pre-incident operating rates potentially achieved in 2027.

data.title: TaskUS board vote on take-private adjourned until Oct. 8
data.description:
TaskUS (NASDAQ:TASK) said the shareholder vote for its planned $16.50 a share take-private deal has been adjourned again and is now scheduled for Oct. 8.

The special meeting is being adjourned again, based on a preliminary assessment of votes received by Wednesday, the unaffiliated stockholder vote had not been obtained, according to a statement on Wednesday.

The vote adjournment comes as proxy adviser ISS has said holders should reject the offer and some investors have also said they plan to reject the offer at its current price of $16.50 a share.

The latest adjournment notice drops language from a Sept. 10 press release that the buyer group refused to bump its offer for TaskUS.

data.title: UBS sees GM regaining 8-10% margins despite tariff drag
data.description:
As tariff headwinds weigh on the company’s margins, UBS sees a path for General Motors North America (GMNA) to restore its profit margin back to the 8-10% range through fixed cost cuts, price increases, and regulatory savings. 

UBS analyst Joseph Spak upgraded GM (NYSE:GM) to Buy from Neutral and raised his price target by 45% to a Street-high of $81, citing the automaker’s historical sales mix, and its tendency to shift toward higher-margin vehicles during certain industrial cycles.

With core GMNA EBIT likely running closer to $9B to $12.3B in 2025, and assuming flat revenue growth, along with the company running at the margin midpoint of UBS’s normalized range post-tariff adjustments, GM (NYSE:GM) would need to make up for ~$3.2B in EBIT to get back to the midpoint of its original 8-10% target and $1.7B in EBT to get to the low end of this target.

Spak believes GMNA can reach this somewhat ambitious goal and restore margins back to 8-10% driven by a number of factors: 

  • Tariff relief from South Korea and Mexico—while still burdensome – could contribute a potential 150 basis point tailwind;
  • Lower emissions standards which will reduce the company’s regulatory expense and contribute a ~$300M credit expense tailwind in 2025;
  • A new fixed cost savings program, $1B of which would be worth 60 basis points to margins; and finally,
  • Continued rate cuts that will add 50-100 basis points to potential price increase leverage (lower rates would keep car payments unchanged and given GM more leeway to hike prices) and add $750M to $1.5B in EBIT.

“Despite tariff cost headwinds, we still believe that an 8-10% margin [for GM North America] is achievable within the next 12-24 months and that the low end of the range could be reached as early as next year,” Spak said in his note to clients.

The upgrade fueled a 2.4% gain in GM (NYSE:GM) shares during premarket trading, setting the stock up to snap a three-day losing streak.

data.title: Marvell Technology announces additional $5B stock repurchase authorization and $1B accelerated share repurchase program
data.description:
• Marvell Technology (NASDAQ:MRVL) announced on Wednesday that its Board of Directors has authorized a new $5 billion stock repurchase program.
  •  As of August 2, 2025, the company had approximately $2 billion remaining under its prior repurchase authorization. Since then, Marvell has repurchased $300 million of common stock in the current quarter under its regular program.
  • In addition, the company has entered into an accelerated share repurchase (ASR) agreement with a leading financial institution to repurchase $1 billion of its common stock.
  • "Our strong balance sheet provides us the flexibility to continue investing in long-term growth, particularly as we pursue the large and expanding opportunity in accelerated infrastructure for AI," Matt Murphy, Chairman and CEO of Marvell.
  • MRVL +3.85% premarket to $77.5.
  • Source: Press Release
